name: Web Awesome
description: The biggest open-source library of highly customizable, framework-agnostic web components from Font Awesome

description.value: The biggest open-source library of highly customizable, framework-agnostic web components from Font Awesome

license.comment: Open source MIT license for Web Awesome Free; Web Awesome Pro available as paid tier

active-maintenance.comment: Active maintenance; v3.1.0 released December 16, 2025; 8 releases total

initial-release.comment: Web Awesome 3.0 officially released April 2025 as the successor to Shoelace

github-stars.comment: 762 stars as of December 2025; relatively new but backed by Font Awesome; inherits Shoelace's 13,800+ star codebase

framework.comment: Framework-agnostic Web Components that work with any framework; React 19+ native support with TypeScript definitions; works with Vue, Angular, Svelte

styling-approach.comment: Uses Shadow DOM with CSS custom properties (--wa- prefix) for theming; CSS Parts for targeted customization

component-model.comment: Styled component library with 11 prebuilt themes; not headless

typescript.comment: Built with TypeScript (64% TypeScript codebase); type definitions provided for React components

ssr-compatible.comment: SSR support via proper import paths; uses /dist for bundlers (Webpack, Vite) and /dist-cdn for direct browser loading

rsc-compatible.comment: Limited RSC compatibility due to Web Components' inherent SSR challenges; requires client-side rendering for interactive components

design-system.comment: Custom design system with 11 themes (Default Awesome, Shoelace, Brutalist, Glossy, Matter, Mellow, Playful, Premium, Tailspin, etc.)

dark-mode.comment: Built-in dark mode via wa-dark class; supports system preference detection via prefers-color-scheme; all 11 themes include light and dark modes

theming-system.comment: Comprehensive theming via CSS custom properties; tokens for colors, typography, spacing, borders, shadows, transitions

css-variables.comment: Extensive use of CSS custom properties with --wa- prefix; organized into borders, colors, focus, shadows, space, transitions, typography

design-tokens.comment: Design tokens organized into categories: Borders, Color, Focus, Shadows, Space, Transitions, Typography, Component Groups

rtl-support.comment: Inherited from Shoelace; RTL support via dir attribute; handled through @shoelace-style/localize library

component-count.comment: 50+ components in Free tier; additional Pro-only components available (Combobox, Page, data grid, date picker coming)

text-input.comment: wa-input component with variants for password, email, search, etc.

textarea.comment: wa-textarea component for multi-line text input

select.comment: wa-select component for single-select dropdown with wa-option

multi-select.comment: wa-select supports multiple selection via multiple attribute

combobox.comment: wa-combobox component available in Pro tier; searchable/filterable select with autocomplete

checkbox.comment: wa-checkbox component with indeterminate state support

radio.comment: wa-radio and wa-radio-group components for radio button groups

switch-toggle.comment: wa-switch component for boolean toggle values

slider.comment: wa-slider component for range slider input

number-input.comment: Input component supports type='number' but no dedicated component with increment/decrement controls

tag-input.comment: No dedicated tag input component; wa-tag exists for display only

color-picker.comment: wa-color-picker component with full color selection UI

date-picker.comment: Not yet available; date picker listed as coming to Pro tier

file-upload.comment: Not provided; use native file input

rating-input.comment: wa-rating component for star rating input

input-groups.comment: Not provided as distinct component; can use slots in custom implementations

split-button.comment: Not listed in component list; may need to compose with button and dropdown

table.comment: Not provided; use native HTML table

data-table.comment: Data grid listed as coming to Pro tier

tree-view.comment: wa-tree and wa-tree-item components with hierarchical structure, keyboard navigation

list-view.comment: Not provided as styled list component

avatar.comment: wa-avatar component with image and fallback support

badge.comment: wa-badge component for small status indicators

tag-chip-pill.comment: wa-tag component for standalone labels with removable option

alert.comment: wa-callout component for static alert/banner messages with variants

toast-notification.comment: No dedicated toast component; use callout or dialog for notifications

progress-circle.comment: wa-progress-ring component for circular progress indicator

spinner-loader.comment: wa-spinner component for indeterminate loading indicator

skeleton.comment: wa-skeleton component for content placeholder during loading

modal-dialog.comment: wa-dialog component with focus management and accessibility

drawer-sheet.comment: wa-drawer component for slide-in panel from edge

popover.comment: wa-popover and wa-popup components for floating content anchored to trigger

tooltip.comment: wa-tooltip component for hover hint text

dropdown-menu.comment: wa-dropdown and wa-dropdown-item components for action menus

context-menu.comment: No dedicated right-click context menu component

tabs.comment: wa-tab-group, wa-tab, wa-tab-panel for horizontal tab-based content switching

vertical-tabs.comment: Tab group supports vertical placement via placement attribute

breadcrumb.comment: wa-breadcrumb and wa-breadcrumb-item for hierarchical navigation with RTL support

navigation-menu.comment: No dedicated navigation menu component; use dropdown for navigation patterns

sidebar.comment: Not provided; use drawer or custom implementation

grid.comment: Not provided as component; CSS utilities may be available

collapsible.comment: wa-details component for expandable/collapsible content sections

splitter.comment: wa-split-panel for resizable split panes with keyboard and mouse control

form-libraries.comment: No official form library bindings; components work with standard forms and Constraint Validation API

animation-libraries.comment: Built-in wa-animation component with presets based on Animate.css; uses Web Animations API

icon-libraries.comment: wa-icon component with Font Awesome integration (same parent company); supports registering custom icon libraries

date-libraries.comment: No date picker component; includes wa-format-date, wa-format-bytes, wa-format-number, wa-relative-time utilities using Intl APIs

aria-compliant.comment: Components built with WAI-ARIA patterns; handle ARIA attributes internally via Shadow DOM

keyboard-navigation.comment: Full keyboard navigation support built into components

screen-reader-tested.comment: Tested with screen readers; strives for AA contrast; some known issues being addressed in cards, tabs

focus-management.comment: Focus management including focus trapping in dialogs; focus states styled via design tokens

wcag-level.comment: Strives for AA contrast and follows ARIA APG guidelines; no specific WCAG level documented; acknowledges accessibility as ongoing effort

accessibility-rating.comment: Adequate - ARIA-compliant with keyboard navigation and screen reader testing; some known issues with tabs, cards announced as groups; team actively improving

bundle-size-full.comment: Package size is 5.73 MB unpacked; components designed for tree-shaking via individual imports

bundle-size-core.comment: Not applicable - individual components imported separately; no minimal core bundle concept

tree-shakeable.comment: Tree-shakeable when importing individual components: import '@awesome.me/webawesome/dist/components/button/button.js'

zero-runtime.comment: JavaScript runtime required for Web Components functionality; built with Lit

lazy-loading.comment: CDN autoloader lazy loads components on demand; individual component imports support code splitting

documentation-quality.comment: Excellent - comprehensive API docs with live examples, theming guides, design tokens reference, accessibility docs, framework integration guides

interactive-docs.comment: Documentation includes live interactive component examples with theme/color scheme switching

storybook.comment: No Storybook; development and demos occur within the documentation site

figma-kit.comment: Official Figma Design Kit available with components, Text Node, Focus Offset, color variables; includes Breadcrumb, Dialog, Drawer, Dropdown, Tooltip, Tree, and more

cli-generator.comment: No CLI for users; fundamentally a component library, not a generator like shadcn

npm-weekly-downloads.comment: 7,191 weekly downloads as of December 2025; relatively new package (launched April 2025)

commercial-support.comment: Web Awesome Pro subscription includes pro-exclusive components, theme builder, pattern library, Figma kit, human support; backed by Font Awesome

style-override.comment: CSS Parts via ::part() selector for component internals; CSS custom properties for global and component-scoped styling; no className override due to Shadow DOM

compound-components.comment: Uses compound component pattern (e.g., wa-tab-group with wa-tab and wa-tab-panel, wa-tree with wa-tree-item)

render-props.comment: Web Components use slots rather than render props pattern

slots.comment: Extensive use of default and named slots for content injection (inherited from Web Components standard)

unstyled-mode.comment: Not unstyled - components ship with complete visual design in 11 themes; not a headless library
